content.text:
Software Design Methods & Design Patterns
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEF12  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Master the fundamental concepts of software design
Understand and apply different advanced design methods
Know how to choose and use modelling and design tools appropriate to the type of application
Understand software quality issues and aspects related to security and maintainability
Recommended prior knowledge:
Software engineering and object-oriented programming.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Review of Design Methods
Software life cycle models (V-model, agile, formal transformations, etc.)
Notions of software quality (reliability, maintainability, performance)
Advanced Object-Oriented Design
Review of OO concepts (inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, etc.)
Design patterns for reuse and modularity
UML modelling tools, UP (Unified Process) RUP, 2TUP
CASE tools in development (examples)
Model-Driven Modelling and Design (MDA)
Model-Driven Architecture (MDA)
Platform models (PIM, PSM)
Model transformations
Code generation tools from models
Quality and Security
Static and dynamic code analysis
Unit and integration testing
Software security techniques (vulnerability prevention, encryption, etc.)
Maintainability (modularity, coupling, cohesion)
Advanced Agile Methods
Advanced Scrum (sprints, roles, planning, etc.)
Kanban (visualisation, flow, etc.)
Adapting agile methods to different contexts
Integrating agility into classic development cycles
Specific Software Design: Generalities
Embedded systems
Cloud
Distributed systems
Databases
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Booch, G., Rumbaugh, J., Jacobson, I. (1999).
The Unified Modeling Language User Guide
. Addison-Wesley.
Fowler, M. (2003).
Patterns of Enterprise Application Architecture
. Addison-Wesley.
Gamma, E., Helm, R., Johnson, R., Vlissides, J. (1995).
Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software
. Addison-Wesley.
OMG (2017).
OMG Unified Modeling Language (OMG UML), Version 2.5.1
. Object Management Group.
Kruchten P. (2001).
Introduction au Rational Unified Process (RUP)
. Editions Eyrolles, Paris.
